L1.area: Cryptographic operation wrappers and data buffer management for native library bindings

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in a Go wrapper for a native cryptographic library. The Go garbage collector can prematurely finalize and deallocate Go objects that hold references to native C library resources while the native code is still operating on them. This can lead to a crash or potential code execution during cryptographic signature verification operations.

L3.area: GPGME context and data handle lifecycle management in the Go-to-C bridge layer
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in a Go wrapper for the GPGME library where the Go garbage collector can prematurely finalize and deallocate Go objects wrapping native GPGME context and data handles while C code is still operating on them. This is particularly exploitable during GPG signature verification operations used by container image pull workflows, leading to a crash or potential arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: Go wrapper objects (Context, Data, Key) can be garbage-collected and freed while the underlying C GPGME library is still using pointers to them, because runtime.KeepAlive is not called after passing Go-managed handles to C functions.
